urgency.message: 3 critical CVE(s) and 7 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: Unpatched Windows Search URI Vulnerability Lets Attackers Steal NTLMv2 Hashes
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an unpatched issue that could be exploited to disclose a user's NTLMv2 hash to the attacker.

Like in the case of CVE-2026-33829, which impacted the Windows Snipping Tool's ms-screensketch: URI handler, the newly flagged issue resides in the search

items.title: Police dismantles 9 crime groups in illegal streaming crackdown
items.summary: European and international law enforcement agencies have dismantled nine organized crime groups and arrested 29 suspects in a major crackdown on illegal streaming operations. [...]

items.title: Infosecurity Europe: How to Get Boards to Prioritize Cyber Risk Quantification
items.summary: Cybersecurity leaders major companies discuss how they got support from the board on cyber risk

items.title: New cyber force would cost up to $11 billion to start, commission says
items.summary: The military branch would take 12 to 18 months to get up and running and also include roughly 5,000 members of the National Guard and up to 6,000 civilians, according to the commission.

items.title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
items.summary: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools.

items.title: Microsoft Tries to Calm Legal Threat Fears After Zero-Day Disclosure Backlash
items.summary:
Microsoft responds to backlash over its threats of legal action against researchers who publicly disclose zero-day vulnerabilities.
The post Microsoft Tries to Calm Legal Threat Fears After Zero-Day Disclosure Backlash appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google adds Android protection against AI deepfake scam calls
items.summary: Google is introducing a new Android security feature that will detect and flag phone calls in which scammers use artificial intelligence to impersonate a user's personal contacts. [...]

items.summary: The emergence of AI models capable to autonomously find and fix vulnerabilities at scale is having a significant impact on patching management, experts say

items.title: CVE-2025-60876 BusyBox wget thru 1.3.7 accepted raw CR (0x0D)/LF (0x0A) and other C0 control bytes in the HTTP request-target (path/query), allowing the request line to be split and attacker-controlled headers to be injected. To preserve the HTTP/1.1 request-line shape METHOD SP request-target SP HTTP/1.1, a raw space (0x20) in the request-target must also be rejected (clients should use %20).
items.summary: Information published.

items.title: New HTTP/2 Bomb Vulnerability Allows Remote DoS on NGINX, Apache, IIS, Envoy & Cloudflare
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a remote denial-of-service exploit that affects major web servers, including NGINX, Apache HTTPD, Microsoft IIS, Envoy, and Cloudflare Pingora.

The vulnerability has been codenamed HTTP/2 Bomb by Calif.

"The vulnerable behavior exists in each server's defa

items.title: Infosecurity Europe: Execs Must Treat Cyber Threats as Statecraft, ISACA Expert Say
items.summary: Private firms are being targeted by nation-state groups for reasons beyond finance, argued ISACA’s Bharat Thakrar

items.title: VS Code zero-day lets hackers steal GitHub tokens in one click
items.summary: A security researcher has released exploit code for a Visual Studio Code (VS Code) zero-day vulnerability that allows attackers to steal GitHub authentication tokens by tricking users into clicking a link. [...]

items.title: Weedhack Attacks Minecraft Users, CountLoader Hits 86K, Miners Spread via Pirated Content
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new campaign targeting Minecraft players via YouTube to spread malware capable of gaining control of victims' systems.

The Minecraft-focused malware-as-a-service (MaaS) campaign has been codenamed Weedhack by McAfee Labs, stating the activity has been active

items.title: Risky Business #840 -- Microsoft walks back researcher threats
items.summary:
On this week’s show special guest co-host Andy Boyd joins Patrick Gray and James Wilson to discuss the week’s cybersecurity news. Andy is the CEO of REDLattice, which makes the Paragon “intelligence collection and reconnaissance” solution.

They cover:


  Adversaries are tracking US troop locations

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9956, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
Microsoft's Coreutils project brings Linux commands to Windows
items.summary: Microsoft announced today at its Build 2026 developer conference the release of Coreutils for Windows, bringing many commonly used Linux command-line utilities to Windows as native applications. [...]

items.title: OpenAI upgrades GPT-5.5, as it plans to retire legacy ChatGPT models
items.summary: OpenAI says it's rolling out a new update that improves the existing GPT-5.5 Instant model, and this move comes ahead of the scheduled retirement of multiple legacy models, including o3. [...]

items.title: Critical Kirki flaw exploited to hijack WordPress admin accounts
items.summary: Hackers are exploiting a critical privilege escalation vulnerability (CVE-2026-8206) in the Kirki plugin for WordPress to take over any user account, including those belonging to administrators. [...]

items.title: Over 116,000 Minecraft systems infected in WeedHack malware campaign
items.summary: A large-scale malware campaign dubbed WeedHack is targeting Minecraft players and has infected more than 116,000 systems since January. [...]

items.title: Zoom CISO: AI as Security Enabler, Not Role-Replacer
items.summary: As Zoom's CISO, Sandra McLeod, discusses the challenges of securing a global communication platform, the promise of AI-driven security workflows, and advice for aspiring cybersecurity leaders.

items.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
items.summary: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.

items.title: DriveSurge Hijacks Thousands of Sites for ClickFix, FakeUpdate Attacks
items.summary: A sneaky, wide-scale IAB operation uses a malicious traffic distribution system (TDS) to redirect visitors of trusted websites to ones that deliver malware.

items.title: Trump Signs Executive Order That Invites Vetting of Top AI Models for National Security Risks
items.summary:
The order establishes a framework for the federal government to vet the national security risks of the most advanced AI systems for up to a month before their public release.
The post Trump Signs Executive Order That Invites Vetting of Top AI Models for National Security Risks appeared first on Secu

items.title: AI-built ransomware toolkit automates EDR evasion, AD discovery
items.summary: A threat actor is using an AI-built ransomware attack toolkit that automates Active Directory discovery and helps evade endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions. [...]

items.title: China Uses Dual-Method Cyberattack on Czech Orgs
items.summary: China is stealing data from high-value targets via a sneaky, double-layer spear-phishing campaign that includes the Azureveil malware.

items.title: Securing AI Agents Before They Go Rogue Is Next to Impossible
items.summary: High-autonomy agents with broad permissions and unfettered access are a recipe for disaster, and enterprises need to act now before they become the next horror story.

items.title: Google June 2026 Android Update Patches 124 Flaws, One Actively Exploited
items.summary:
Google on Monday released patches for 124 security vulnerabilities impacting its Android operating system for the month of June 2026, including one high-severity flaw in the Framework component that has come under active exploitation.

Tracked as CVE-2025-48595 (CVSS score: 8.4), the security flaw h

items.title: White House unveils pared-back AI executive order
items.summary: The order notes that federal access to the models should be subject to “appropriate confidentiality, cybersecurity, insider-risk, and intellectual-property protection, use, and nondisclosure requirements.”

items.title: Gamaredon Exploits WinRAR to Deliver GammaWorm and GammaSteel Against Ukraine
items.summary:
The Russian hacking group known as Gamaredon has been attributed to the continued exploitation of a WinRAR vulnerability to deliver multiple malware families aimed at data theft and propagation.

Per Sekoia, the activity involves the weaponization of CVE-2025-8088, a path traversal flaw in WinRAR, t

items.title: Oracle WebLogic CVE-2024-21182 Added to KEV Catalog After Active Exploitation
items.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a high-severity security flaw impacting Oracle WebLogic Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.

The vulnerability, CVE-2024-21182 (CVSS score: 7.5), allows

items.title: Android Is Fighting Phone Scams With a New Feature to Prove Who’s Calling
items.summary: Available for Android 12 and later, the anti-scam feature is baked into Google Dialer, which sends a silent “confirmation signal” to ensure whoever’s calling you is who they appear to be.

items.title: The npm Threat Landscape: Attack Surface and Mitigations (Updated June 2)

items.title: Two New Reports Offer Competing Explanations for Cybersecurity’s Growing Crisis
items.summary:
As AI shortens the path from vulnerability disclosure to exploitation, researchers disagree on whether the problem is inadequate security tools or inadequate operational control.
The post Two New Reports Offer Competing Explanations for Cybersecurity’s Growing Crisis appeared first on Security

items.title: Russia claims foreign spy agencies hacked officials' phones
items.summary: In a statement, Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB) said it had uncovered what it described as a "large-scale operation" involving malicious software installed on the mobile devices of senior Russian officials.

items.summary: NCSC director of operations, Paul Chichester, says it’s time to future-proof cybersecurity today

items.title: Exclusive: How One Line of Code Put Billions of Microsoft Android App Downloads at Risk
items.summary:
A simple development setting bypassed protections designed to prevent unauthorized Android apps from accessing Microsoft account tokens, exposing billions of installations.
